---
title: "Required Events in Time‑to‑Event Designs"
subtitle: "*Numerical comparison across East, nQuery, and R*"
image: "../man/figures/events.png"
---

This short note complements the comparison of sample size calculations by focusing specifically on the **required number of events** for time‑to‑event designs. Using the same scenarios and assumptions, we report the numerical results obtained from East, nQuery, and R implementations, highlighting how closely the tools agree—both for fixed designs and group‑sequential settings. The tables and plots below summarize these event‑based results.

```{r}
#| label: setup
source("../quarto-setup.R")
```

```{r}
fixed <- read_rds("../outputs/comp_surv_fixed.rds")
gs <- read_rds("../outputs/comp_surv_gs.rds")
```

## Fixed Design

::: {.light-content}
```{r}
#| out-width: 100%
gt_theme_flatly(fixed$tables$table_e_ratio)
fixed$plots$p_e_ratio + theme_flatly()
```
:::

::: {.dark-content}
```{r}
#| out-width: 100%
gt_theme_darkly(fixed$tables$table_e_ratio) 
fixed$plots$p_e_ratio + theme_darkly()
```
:::

## Group-Sequential Design 

::: {.light-content}
```{r}
#| out-width: 100%
gt_theme_flatly(gs$tables$table_e_ratio)
gs$plots$p_e_ratio + theme_flatly()
```
:::

::: {.dark-content}
```{r}
#| out-width: 100%
gt_theme_darkly(gs$tables$table_e_ratio) 
gs$plots$p_e_ratio + theme_darkly()
```
:::
